purpose: Machine-readable prompt ladder for the active three-part amber-removal and premium VS Code restyle workstream. Part 1 covers the complete Urban Analytics modal (A01-A10). Part 2 covers the Center Panel shell + every tab interior excluding the Map Explorer files (C01-C10) and preserves existing atmospheric header animations. Part 3 covers the complete Map Explorer (B01-B10). The ledger is the execution source of truth.

historicalBaseline.description: The previous broad 38-prompt rollout is superseded for future execution. Prompts 00-17 were completed on 2026-05-15 and remain historical token/shared-shell baseline.

requiredPreflight: Read COLOR_SYSTEM_UNIT_MATRIX.md for the active unit.
Read COLOR_SYSTEM_TOKEN_REFERENCE.md for allowed token families.
Read only the active prompt block and the current ledger status.
Keep edits color, chrome, and density scoped.
requiredValidation: Run typecheck when TS or TSX changes.
Run targeted map tests when Map Explorer behavior-adjacent files or color assertions change.
Run the active-scope amber scan before and after implementation.
Record exact validation results in the ledger.
guardrails: Do not use color alone for state.
Do not make demo, unknown, stale, blocked, invalid, residual-gap, environment-dependent, or deferred output look ready.
Keep data palettes separate from UI chrome/status colors.
Do not move or archive DEVELOPMENT_PLANS during color work.
No amber/gold/yellow/orange UI chrome in Urban Analytics modal or Map Explorer.
Remove unnecessary card frames and filled button plates in the active scope.
